titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Strobe Talbott Papers
titlestmt.author: Nicholas Wantsala and Stephanie Bredbenner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday March 25, 2026 at 23:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Chiefly in English. Some materials in Russian.

did.physdesc: 2 CDR-Ws (1.22 gigabytes) and 2 3.5 inch floppy disks (0.002 gigabytes)
did.abstract: The papers include Talbott's diaries, correspondence, speeches, research materials, notebooks, photographs, audiovisual recordings, and computer media, that together provide extensive insight into Talbott's evolving views of post-Cold War diplomacy, including sanctions, missile proliferation, the Helsinki Summit, and NATO expansion.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research. Boxes 25-26 (computer media and audiovisual material): restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Early life and career, 1963-1993. II. Government career, 1991-2001. III. Post-government career, 2001-2019. IV. Writings, 1968-2003. V. Personal and family papers, 1950-2004. VI. Computer media and sound recordings.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Strobe Talbott Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Strobe Talbott papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Yale Archival Processing Manual. The collection was put into its current arrangement during processing in 2025.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Most folder titles have been devised by the archivist. The creator's original folder titles have been enclosed in quotation marks.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Nelson Strobridge "Strobe" Talbott III is an American foreign policy analyst, diplomat, and former deputy secretary of State, serving from 1994 to 2001. Talbott was born on April 25, 1946, in Dayton, Ohio to Helen Josephine (Large) and Nelson Strobridge "Bud" Talbott II. He graduated from Yale University in 1968, where he was the chairman of the Yale Daily News . Talbott was a Rhodes Scholar at the University of Oxford, where he befriended future President Bill Clinton and translated Nikita Khrushchev's memoirs into English. Talbott worked as a journalist for Time magazine before becoming ambassador-at-large at the State Department from 1993 to 1994. He served as deputy secretary of state from 1994 to 2001. After leaving government service, Talbott was president of the Brookings Institute from 2002 to 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the diplomatic, political, and scholarly career of Strobe Talbott, including his government service, journalism career, and writings, particularly related to Russia. The papers include diaries, correspondence, speeches, research materials, photographs, audiovisual recordings, computer media, and notebooks that together provide extensive insight into Talbott's evolving views of post-Cold War diplomacy, including sanctions, missile proliferation, the Helsinki Summit, and NATO expansion.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into subseries: Hotchkiss School, Yale and Oxford and Time magazine.

did.unittitle: "Gullible's Travels: A Letter to My Friends About My Month in Moscow"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A document, dated 1993, certifying that Talbott received a master's degree from Oxford. The original diploma is not present in the collection.

did.unittitle: "Reaction to Time column Clinton and the draft"
Clippings on Yeltsin, Bush, START II signing

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Diaries, Speeches and public remarks, Correspondence and memoranda, Working files, research materials, and notes, and Photographs.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Talbott maintained his diaries in a digital format and later printed them out and placed the hard copies in binders. Binder dividers have been retained.

Some journal entries contain an incorrect date of May 2, 2022 due to an encoding error, possibly because that was the date Talbott printed the hard copies of the diary entries. Researchers should be aware that certain timestamps may be missing, misaligned, or encoded inconsistently with surrounding materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Talbott's diaries log his daily activities, relate the proceedings of meetings, and record his thoughts and ideas about U.S. diplomatic policy. They are often formatted as letters to his parents, particularly his father. Talbott organized his diaries into nine volumes: Volume I (August 1991-June 1994), Volume II (June 1994-April 1995), Volume III (April 1995-January 1996), Volume IV (January 1996-January 1997), Volume V (January 1997-February 1998), Volume VI (February 1998-December 1998), Volume VII (December 1998-April 1999), Volume VIII (April 1999-December 1999), and Volume IX (December 1999-November 2000). Each volume begins with a heading listing the dates covered in the entries.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: Outgoing and Incoming, and chronologically therein.

did.unittitle: Talbott to Warren Christopher re: State appointment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter Talbott wrote to Bill Clinton shortly before the 1992 presidential election.

did.unittitle: Talbott memo to Warren Christopher and Bill Clinton
Working files, research materials, and notes
Research material and notes on Russia
House and Senate hearings with Defense personnel

odd.head: General
odd.p: Eric Edelman was Talbott's executive assistant at the State Department.

did.unittitle: House International Relations Committee hearing on Russian crisis
Joung Hoo Lee Brookings photo album
Foreign Service Institute Library event sign
Mayakovsky Agonistes: The Myth of the Self in the Poetry of Vladimir Mayakovsky (Oxford dissertation)
The Russia Hand chapter draft with Warren Christopher's comments

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Sun Valley Writers Conference The World in 2010: Clear and Present Dangers Strobe Talbott
Sun Valley Writers Conference A Talk by the Author and Diplomant Strobe Talbott

did.physdesc: 1.47 Megabytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.
1.49 Megabytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Clinton's foreign policy in Russia, Anne Garrels NPR
